countries/kuwait.summary: IRAN ATTACK (Jun 28) — DAY 2 + DOHA TALKS (Jun 30): US-Iran talks in Doha June 30 directly affect Kuwait security. Tanker traffic resuming but Strait still contested; Iran still claims closure authority. Kuwait desalination plants (99% of drinking water) remain vulnerable. No new attack on Kuwait June 30. Doha talks outcome will determine ceasefire durability for Kuwait. Kuwait never assessed — assessment critical given geographic exposure and water security.

countries/bahrain.summary: IRAN ATTACK (Jun 28) — DAY 2 + DOHA TALKS (Jun 30): No new Iranian attack on Bahrain June 30; stand-down holding. US-Iran Doha talks (Jun 30) directly affect Bahrain security given Fifth Fleet HQ presence. Amnesty International June 2026 research on potential war crimes from Iran strikes on Bahrain/Saudi Arabia remains current. 28+ civilians killed across GCC from cumulative Iranian strikes. Bahrain never assessed — assessment required urgently.

countries/nigeria.summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK — CONTINUING (Jun 30, Day 15+): 35M+ at IPC Phase 3+ — highest ever recorded in Nigeria. 15,000+ at Phase 5 Catastrophe in Borno State. FAO-WFP Hunger Hotspots: highest-concern global hotspot. 52.8M food insecure across West Africa/Sahel (Jun-Aug). WFP funding crisis ongoing. No new development Jun 30 — situation at peak severity, continuing from prior scan.

countries/lebanon.summary: POST-CEASEFIRE CASUALTIES (Jun 30): 83 killed / 141 wounded in Israeli attacks the day the renewed ceasefire was announced — most casualties in southern Lebanon. 100+ Israeli airstrikes since midnight of ceasefire. Israeli drone struck Nabatieh the day after the framework agreement was signed. Hezbollah rejected framework as "humiliation, disgrace, surrender." US Secretary Rubio called the Jun 28 deal "the beginning of the beginning" but ceasefire is not holding on Day 2. 4,000+ total killed in Lebanon since Mar 2, 2026. 1.2M+ displaced. Framework durability in serious question.

countries/venezuela.summary: EARTHQUAKE — UPDATED TOLL (Jun 30, Day 6): Death toll confirmed at 1,719 (up from 1,450 Jun 28), with NYT noting this may be "substantial undercount." 46,600 reported missing. 3,150+ injured. US commitment doubled to $300M (was $150M Jun 29). UNICEF second relief shipment (48 MT from Copenhagen) due today. Survivors still being found in rubble. Ongoing at crisis severity; government accountability concerns persist. Already-priced event — new figures only.

countries/qatar.summary: DOHA TALKS HOST (Jun 30 — NEW): Qatar is hosting US-Iran technical talks on June 30, the pivotal moment in the Islamabad Memorandum implementation. Gharibabadi (Iran) meeting separately with Qatari mediators; Witkoff (US) also in Doha. Qatar mediating Strait of Hormuz management talks. Desalination plants — 99% of Qatar drinking water — remain at risk under declared Iranian Hormuz closure. Qatar Al Udeid Air Base targeted in prior Iran war strikes. Never assessed — mediator role and ongoing war risk context both material.

fortune-500/unitedhealth-group.summary: DOJ CRIMINAL + CIVIL PROBE — CONTINUING (Jun 30): Criminal and civil DOJ probes into Medicare Advantage overbilling, Optum Rx billing, antitrust. UHG launched third-party review of billing practices. DOJ Jun 23 healthcare fraud takedown ($6.5B, 455 defendants) maintains sector scrutiny. No new specific UHG development Jun 30. Probe ongoing at high severity.

countries/israel.summary: POST-CEASEFIRE STRIKES INTENSIFY (Jun 30): Lebanon Ministry of Public Health reports 83 killed / 141 wounded in Israeli attacks on Friday, the day of the renewed ceasefire — most casualties in southern Lebanon, with 100+ Israeli airstrikes since midnight of ceasefire. Israeli drone struck Nabatieh the day after the US-brokered framework was signed. Gaza: continuing ceasefire violations (3,338+ violations through Jun 20); 77% of population with acute food insecurity; only 36% of allocated aid trucks entered. ICC arrest warrants active. UN Commission Jun 23 finding of genocide/atrocity crimes stands. Ongoing at critical severity.

countries/burkina-faso.summary: LEAN SEASON PEAK — CONTINUING (Jun 30): 52.8M food insecure across West Africa/Sahel. Burkina Faso hosts largest IDP population after Nigeria. JNIM attacks continue; besieged population 3.5M+. 28% of $3.7B regional appeal funded. No new development Jun 30 — situation ongoing at peak severity.

countries/democratic-republic-of-c.summary: EBOLA SPREADS TO 4TH PROVINCE (Jun 30 — NEW): DRC now 1,307 confirmed cases / 377 deaths, up from 1,155/304 reported Jun 25 (first updated count published Jun 29-30). Geographic escalation: spread to Haut-Uele province (borders South Sudan and Central African Republic) after infected person travelled from Bunia (Ituri). DRC entire northeast (15M people) now affected. Uganda: 19 confirmed cases (up from ~7 when borders closed May 27); WHO commends Uganda response but urges regional vigilance. Bundibugyo strain — no vaccine or specific treatment. Fastest-growing Ebola outbreak in recorded history. Cross-border risk to South Sudan, CAR, Rwanda elevated.

countries/saudi-arabia.summary: DOHA TALKS JUN 30 — CONTEXT: US-Iran Doha talks June 30 directly affect ceasefire durability for Saudi Arabia. Amnesty International June 2026 research on potential war crimes from Iran strikes on Bahrain/Saudi Arabia remains current. Saudi Arabia last assessed Apr 20. Assessment should incorporate ongoing Iran war civilian impact and Doha outcome.

countries/iran.summary: DOHA TALKS JUN 30 — IRAN DOES NOT CONFIRM (Day 1 of talks): Trump announced technical talks in Doha; senior Iranian negotiator Gharibabadi said reports were "not confirmed," though consultations with Qatar continue. US envoy Witkoff arrived in Doha; meeting format: Nick Stewart (US) and Gharibabadi separately with Qatari/Pakistani mediators, not direct talks. Focus shifted to Strait of Hormuz management (not nuclear). Tanker traffic resuming but hundreds of vessels still stranded; central channel still mined; Iran still demanding transit fees under declared closure. Islamabad Memorandum 60-day clock continues. Execution surge: 784+ YTD (37-year high). Hunger strikes Week 126+ across 56 prisons.

countries/yemen.summary: WFP CRISIS CONTINUING (Jun 30): WFP terminated all 365 staff contracts in Houthi northern Yemen. 73 UN workers detained. 22.3M need assistance. Only 12.7% of $2.16B appeal funded. US-Iran Doha talks Jun 30 (Strait of Hormuz focus) directly affect Yemen aid delivery costs. 18.3M acutely food insecure. No new specific Yemen development Jun 30 beyond ongoing crisis.

countries/sudan.summary: EL OBEID ONGOING (Jun 30 — already priced): SAF-RSF conflict, 500,000+ civilians besieged. Al-Burhan rejected ceasefire Jun 29. Famine confirmed in El-Fasher and Kadugli. US State Dept "imminent risk of mass atrocities." No new major military development Jun 30 beyond Jun 29 scan. Situation ongoing at critical severity.

countries/south-sudan.summary: FAMINE RISK CONTINUING (Jun 30): 7.8M face acute food insecurity (56% of population). 2.2M children acutely malnourished. Four counties at famine risk. 140,000 displaced in Akobo. WFP serving 1.5M fewer than planned. Ebola spread to DRC Haut-Uele (borders South Sudan) elevates cross-border disease risk. No significant new development Jun 30 beyond ongoing crisis.

countries/somalia.summary: BUUR HAKABA FAMINE THRESHOLD CROSSED — CONTINUING (Jun 30 — already priced): IPC AMN Phase 5 (Extremely Critical) in Burhakaba/Buur Hakaba. GAM rate 37.1%. 6M people (1-in-3) at Crisis/worse. WFP reaching approximately 1-in-10. Al-Shabaab attacks ongoing. No new formal IPC/FSNAU famine declaration published as of Jun 30. Situation at peak severity, continuing.

countries/bolivia.summary: STATE OF EMERGENCY RESOLVED JUN 23 — AFTERMATH (Jun 19-29): Bolivia declared state of emergency Jun 19-20 restricting mass gatherings during nationwide protests by miners, teachers, farmers. 17 killed, 365 arrested. Bundibugyo strain — no vaccine or specific treatment.
